Oops, sorry about that guys. Once you've set up your pixel, you can then establish events that are important to you on your website. So whether someone purchased, whether they became a lead, whether they viewed a page. So this is really going to allow you to do the tracking that you need to do inside of the Facebook ads manager. It's also going to allow you to optimize for certain events that are important to you when using the conversion objective, for example. So after someone has visited your website, you want to of course follow up with them with ads to get them to take the action that they haven't taken or maybe to show them more content that you can further build that relationship with them to get them to buy. So if you go to create audience and custom audience, the magic here lives under website traffic and we have lots of options.

## [03:01 - 06:07]

So you can either retarget all website visitors up to 180 days out. You can target people who have visited specific web pages. So maybe someone who has visited a certain blog post, you want to retarget them with another piece of content or you want to retarget them with a product. You can also retarget people by time spent. So you can retarget people who have spent the top 25% most time on your site, the top 10%, the top 5% very valuable for finding those really qualified people who have spent the most time on your website. You can also retarget people based off of events. So page view lead, these are the ones that have been set up so far inside the code and quill ad account. Really, really interesting. So what else is cool is that you can refine these audiences by frequencies. So for example, people who have visited a specific web page four or more times. And as you can see, you could also refine this by device. And when you're setting these up, so say I wanted to reach someone who visited my blog. So let's do digitalmarketer.com forward slash blog. But say I wanted to exclude right here, oops. So say I wanted to exclude people who have visited, people who have purchased. So what I would do is I would have people who have visited the blog, but I would like to exclude people who have purchased. So this is how you do dynamic retargeting throughout your funnel. So for example, if you're sending traffic to an opt-in page where you're asking someone for their email address in exchange for lead magnet and in exchange for something that they see as valuable. And then on the next page, let's say that you're selling them a product. Well let's say that you want to set up a campaign to retarget people who have opted in for that lead magnet, but who haven't purchased that product. Well, what you would do is you would then put people who have visited the thank you page of the lead magnet, right? But then you would exclude people who have purchased. So this is how you set up the dynamic retargeting that allows you to do full funnel retargeting, to catch people who have fallen off on a certain stage of your customer journey and help to

## [06:07 - 06:55]

remind them and help to give them a gentle nudge to move to the next stage of the funnel. So lots of powerful stuff that we can do here with custom audiences to build these retargeting audiences. I want you to think about what audiences can you set up to retarget people, not just who have visited your site and as a whole, but to retarget people to really remind them to take the next steps that you're wanting them to take inside of your customer journey.
